title: Left join with subquery exception on empty tables
description:
### What happens?

Left join with subquery on empty tables causes substract overflow exception:

```cpp
if (!TrySubtractOperator::Operation(left, right, result)) {
	throw OutOfRangeException("Overflow in subtraction of %s (%s - %s)!", TypeIdToString(GetTypeId<TA>()),
			                        NumericHelper::ToString(left), NumericHelper::ToString(right));
}
```


### To Reproduce

```sql
create table t1 (id integer, revision integer, col integer);
create table t2 (id integer, revision integer, col1 integer);

select col, col1 from t1
  left join t2 on t1.id = t2.id
    and t1.revision = t2.revision
where t1.revision = (select max(z.revision) from t1 z where z.id = t1.id);
```

### OS:

Windows

### DuckDB Version:

1.1.2

### DuckDB Client:

C API

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

Aex G

### Affiliation:

none

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a source build

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (id Int32, revision Int32, col Int32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (id, revision);
CREATE TABLE t2 (id Int32, revision Int32, col1 String) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (id, revision);
INSERT INTO t1 (id, revision, col) VALUES (1, 1, 10), (2, 2, 20);
INSERT INTO t2 (id, revision, col1) VALUES (1, 1, 'test1'), (3, 3, 'test3');
SELECT col, col1 FROM t1 LEFT JOIN t2 ON t1.id = t2.id AND t1.revision = t2.revision WHERE t1.revision = (SELECT MAX(z.revision) FROM t1 z WHERE z.id = t1.id);
key_bug_features: Left join with subquery on empty tables
